channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US to pay Moderna $176 million to develop pandemic flu vaccine
item.description: The U.S. government will pay the vaccine maker Moderna $176 million to develop a pandemic vaccine that could be used to treat bird flu in people as cases in dairy cows continue to mount across the country, federal officials announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hungary's Russia-friendly leader is in Ukraine. It's his first visit since the war began
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — During his first visit to neighboring Ukraine since Russia’s full-scale invasion, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán told President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Tuesday that the war was Europe's “most important issue,” and recommended an immediate ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Senator wants Washington Commanders to pay tribute to an old logo that offends many Indigenous
item.description: After a half century of activism, many Native Americans thought a bitter debate over the capital's football mascot was over two years ago when the team became the Washington Commanders.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court won’t review Illinois ban on certain semiautomatic guns
item.description: The Supreme Court declined to hear a challenge to Illinois’s ban on certain semiautomatic weapons and high-capacity magazines on Tuesday, avoiding what would’ve been a blockbuster Second Amendment case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear tech liability challenge from teen groomed on Snapchat
item.description: The Supreme Court will not consider a challenge to the scope of a federal law immunizing tech companies from liability for their users’ content brought by a teenager who was allegedly groomed by a teacher on Snapchat.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: A stampede at a religious event in India has killed at least 60 people
item.description: LUCKNOW, India (AP) — A stampede among thousands of people at a religious gathering in northern India killed at least 60 and left scores injured, officials said Tuesday, adding the toll could rise.
item.dc:creator: BISWAJEET BANERJEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 20 Disneyland park goers stuck at top of roller coaster when ride malfunctions
item.description: Twenty riders on Disney's Incredicoaster at Anaheim's California Adventure had to be rescued after an unknown malfunction, with temperatures in the 80s.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red Lobster rolls out Flavor Flav's Faves menu
item.description: A Red Lobster menu full of Flavor Flav's favorite items? Yeah boyeeeeee! The restaurant chain has rolled out a new menu inspired by its biggest celebrity fan.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Groom shot in the head during wedding party robbery attempt
item.description: A wedding celebration quickly turned to tragedy after a group of masked gunmen crashed the reception and shot the groom..
item.dc:creator: Caroline Pettey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden campaign tries to stamp out Democratic calls to drop out
item.description: President Biden’s reelection campaign and Biden's allies are going to great lengths to stamp out questions from Democrats over whether he should drop out of the race after an abysmal debate performance.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: GOP seizes on Biden debate fallout to batter vulnerable Democrats
item.description: Republicans are seizing on President Biden’s disastrous debate performance last week by tying their Democratic counterparts to the fallout.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: The Supreme Court will hear an appeal to upend Texas' age verification law for porn websites
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Tuesday agreed to hear an appeal from the adult entertainment industry seeking to upend a Texas law that requires pornographic websites to verify the age of their users.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mars and meteors: July skygazing guide
item.description: July promises to be a celestial feast for skygazers, with events ranging from meteor showers to planetary alignments.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh adult industry's challenge to Texas law
item.description: The Supreme Court has indicated it will take up a case next term involving the adult entertainment industry’s challenge to a Texas law requiring pornography sites to implement age-verification measures.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, ABC 4

item.title: Darrell Christian, former AP managing editor and sports editor, dies at 75
item.description: Darrell L. Christian, a former managing editor and sports editor of The Associated Press known for a demanding demeanor and insistence on excellence during more than four decades with the news agency, died Monday. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago Pride parade chaos: police attacked and 53 arrested
item.description: More than 50 people were arrested and multiple police officers were injured following Chicago's Pride parade Sunday with reports of people jumping on vehicles and throwing bottles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens of illegal immigrants accused of border stampede in El Paso, Texas, released into US: ICE
item.description: Dozens of the more than 200 illegal immigrants who stormed the southern border in El Paso, Texas, in March have been released into the U.S., ICE says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arthur Crudup wrote the song that became Elvis' first hit. He barely got paid
item.description: FRANKTOWN, Va. (AP) — Arthur “Big Boy” Crudup helped invent rock ‘n’ roll. His 1946 song “That’s All Right,” an easygoing shrug to a lover, would become the first single Elvis Presley ever released. Rod Stewart would sing it on a chart-topping album. Led Zeppelin would play it live.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cup Noodles releases new s'mores flavor for summer
item.description: Nissin Foods USA announced they're releasing a new limited-edition Cup Noodles Campfire S'mores flavor of ramen noodles.
item.dc:creator: Drew Miller, KRON4

item.title: Devout athletes find strength in their faith. But practicing it and elite sports can pose hurdles
item.description: It’s been 100 years since a Scottish runner famously refused to race on a Sunday at the Paris Olympics because of his Christian beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto And Deepa Bharath, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: White House touts efforts to lower gas prices ahead of July 4 travel blitz
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday the sale of 1 million barrels of gasoline, making its latest effort to reduce gas prices ahead of the Fourth of July travel blitz.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Biden proposes 1st-ever nationwide worker protections from heat
item.description: As the nation sizzles, the Biden administration is proposing the nation’s first-ever standards aimed at protecting workers from extreme heat.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden administration proposes rule for workplaces to address excessive heat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration proposed a new rule Tuesday to address excessive heat in the workplace, as tens of millions of people in the U.S. are under heat advisories due to blistering temperatures. If finalized, the measure would protect an estimated 36 million U.S.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden proposes first-ever nationwide worker protections from heat
item.description: As the nation sizzles, the Biden administration is proposing the nation’s first-ever standards aimed at protecting workers from extreme heat.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Karen Read murder case ends in a mistrial. Prosecutors say they will try again
item.description: DEDHAM, Mass. (AP) — A judge declared a mistrial Monday after jurors deadlocked in the polarizing and much-watched case of Karen Read, a woman accused of striking her Boston police officer boyfriend with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man who confessed to killing 4 people in Maine, including his parents, sentenced to life in prison
item.description: WEST BATH, Maine (AP) — A man who confessed to killing his parents and two of their friends and wounding three people in a highway shooting pleaded guilty to murder and other charges on Monday, and a judge sentenced him to the maximum term of life in prison.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump seeks to set aside hush money verdict hours after immunity ruling
item.description: In prior court filings, Trump contended he is immune from prosecution for conduct alleged to involve official acts during his tenure in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jamie Foxx shares more details about his 2023 medical scare
item.description: Jamie Foxx has shared some new details about the 2023 medical emergency that left him unable to walk.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have broad immunity, dimming chance of a pre-election Trump trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday ruled for the first time that former presidents have broad immunity from prosecution, extending the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss and all but ending prospe
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump seeks to set aside New York verdict hours after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: Donald Trump's lawyers on Monday asked the New York judge who presided over his hush money trial to set aside his conviction and delay his sentencing scheduled for later this month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump golf courses' liquor licenses not renewed after conviction
item.description: The licenses expired Sunday, but the two golf clubs were issued interim permits to serve alcohol until a hearing is held on the matter. The hearing is scheduled for July 19, which the division noted is after Trump’s sentencing on July 11.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sforza, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have some immunity, sends Trump case back to lower court
item.description: In a historic 6-3 ruling, the justices said for the first time that former presidents have absolute immunity from prosecution for their official acts and no immunity for unofficial acts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tracking the Tropics: Beryl strengthens into Category 5 hurricane
item.description: The National Hurricane Center is tracking Hurricane Beryl, Tropical Storm Chris and a new tropical wave.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WGN 9

item.title: Stingray that got pregnant despite no male companion has died, aquarium says
item.description: Video above: New encounter at ZooTampa allows you to get into the water with stingrays HENDERSONVILLE, N.C. (AP) — A stingray that got pregnant at a North Carolina aquarium this winter despite not having shared a tank with a male of her species for many years has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How the hot water that fueled Hurricane Beryl foretells a scary storm season
item.description: Hurricane Beryl’s explosive growth into an unprecedented early storm shows the literal hot water the Atlantic and Caribbean are in right now and the kind of season ahead.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for a North Dakota truck stop in a new blow to federal regulators
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court opened the door Monday to new, broad challenges to regulations long after they take effect, the third blow in a week to federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Supreme Court casts doubt on Florida and Texas laws to regulate social media platforms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday kept on hold efforts by Texas and Florida to limit how Facebook, TikTok, X, YouTube and other social media platforms regulate content posted by their users in a ruling that strongly defended the platforms' free speech rights.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
